b3-p-familiar.note: کمیونٹی نظام خاندان کے افراد کے لیے بہت فائدہ مند ہے۔ درخواست سپین میں داخلے کے 90 دنوں میں دینی ہوگی۔ فارم EX-19۔
opts: 21 سال سے کم یا زیر کفالت بچہ/بچی

b3-r-familiar-ue.text: EU شہری کے خاندان کا رہائشی کارڈ
b3-r-familiar-ue.note: داخلے کے 90 دنوں میں درخواست دیں۔ دستاویزات: فارم EX-19 + درست پاسپورٹ + خاندانی رشتے کی دستاویزات (شادی/پیدائش کا سرٹیفکیٹ apostille شدہ اور ترجمہ شدہ) + EU خاندانی فرد کا سبز NIE۔ فیس: 790 کوڈ 012 (DNI کی قیمت)۔ ابتدائی مدت: 5 سال۔ 5 سال بعد → مستقل رہائش (10 سال، خودکار تجدید)۔ فارم EX-19۔

b3-r-familiar-altres.text: بڑھا ہوا خاندان — ہر کیس کا الگ جائزہ
b3-r-familiar-altres.note: دوسرے رشتہ دار (بھائی بہن، سالے/سالی، بھانجے/بھتیجے...) کو کمیونٹی نظام کا خودکار حق نہیں۔ اصل ملک میں مالی انحصار یا 24 ماہ کی رہائش ثابت کرنی ہوگی۔ فیصلہ extranjeria دفتر کی صوابدید پر ہے۔ ہم ماہر وکیل سے مشورہ تجویز کرتے ہیں۔
opts: EU قانون میں ماہر وکیل سے مشورہ کریں

b3-r-familiar-espanyol.text: ہسپانوی شہری کا خاندان — دو ممکنہ راستے
b3-r-familiar-espanyol.note: اگر آپ کے ہسپانوی رشتہ دار نے آزادانہ نقل و حرکت کا حق استعمال کیا ہے (کسی اور EU ملک میں رہے ہیں)، تو آپ EX-19 (کمیونٹی نظام، بہت فائدہ مند) سے جا سکتے ہیں۔ اگر آزادانہ نقل و حرکت استعمال نہیں کی، تو EX-24 (ہسپانوی کے خاندان کا عام نظام) سے جانا ہوگا۔ شک ہو تو وکیل سے مشورہ کریں — EX-19 راستہ بہت زیادہ فائدہ مند ہے اور تلاش کرنے کے قابل ہے۔
opts: دوسرے EU ملک میں رہے ہیں → EX-19 (کمیونٹی نظام)
کبھی سپین سے باہر نہیں رہے → EX-24 (عام نظام)
بہترین راستہ جاننے کے لیے وکیل سے مشورہ کریں

b4-p0.note: آپ جہاں ہیں وہ طے کرتا ہے کہ آپ کو کیسے آگے بڑھنا ہے۔ آپ کو بین الاقوامی تحفظ کی درخواست کا حق ہے چاہے آپ کہیں بھی ہوں۔
opts: سرحد، بندرگاہ یا ہسپانوی ہوائی اڈے پر

b4-r-frontera.text: سرحد پر پناہ کی درخواست کا حق — یہ سکرین دکھائیں
b4-r-frontera.note: آپ کو کسی بھی داخلی مقام پر بین الاقوامی تحفظ کی درخواست کا حق ہے۔ واضح طور پر کہیں: 'میں بین الاقوامی تحفظ کی درخواست کرتا/کرتی ہوں'۔ جب تک درخواست پر کارروائی ہو رہی ہے آپ کو ملک بدر نہیں کیا جا سکتا۔ آپ کو مفت ترجمان کا حق ہے۔ آپ کو مفت وکیل کا حق ہے۔ درخواست رازدارانہ ہے — آپ کے ملک کو مطلع نہیں کیا جائے گا۔ اگر درخواست مسترد ہو تو نظرثانی کے لیے 2 کاروباری دن ہیں۔
opts: کہیں: میں بین الاقوامی تحفظ کی درخواست کرتا/کرتی ہوں

b4-r-cie.text: CIE میں — آپ یہاں سے بھی پناہ کی درخواست دے سکتے ہیں
b4-r-cie.note: آپ CIE میں رہتے ہوئے بھی بین الاقوامی تحفظ کی درخواست دے سکتے ہیں۔ ڈیوٹی وکیل سے بات کرنے کی درخواست کریں یا ACNUR یا CEAR سے رابطہ کریں۔ آپ کو مفت ترجمان اور وکیل کا حق ہے۔ درخواست کارروائی کے دوران ملک بدری کو روک دیتی ہے۔
opts: CIE عملے سے پناہ کی درخواست کریں

b4-p1.text: کیا آپ نے بین الاقوامی تحفظ کی درخواست دے دی ہے؟
b4-p1.note: پناہ کی درخواست کی مدت اسپین میں داخلے سے 1 مہینہ ہے۔ اگر مہینہ گزر گیا ہے تو آپ مدت بڑھانے کی جائز وجہ پیش کر سکتے ہیں۔ یہ عمل مفت ہے۔
opts: نہیں، ابھی تک درخواست نہیں دی

b4-p-nosol.text: کیا آپ اسپین میں 1 مہینے سے کم عرصہ پہلے داخل ہوئے ہیں؟
b4-p-nosol.note: سرکاری مدت داخلے سے 1 مہینہ ہے۔ مدت گزرنے کے بعد آپ جائز وجہ پیش کر سکتے ہیں۔ یہ عمل مفت اور رازدارانہ ہے۔

b4-r-com-sol.text: اسپین میں بین الاقوامی تحفظ کی درخواست کیسے کریں
b4-r-com-sol.note: کہاں جمع کرائیں: پناہ اور مہاجرین کا دفتر (OAR) — میڈرڈ: C/ Pradillo 40؛ غیر ملکیوں کی خدمات والے پولیس تھانے (کوئی بھی صوبہ)؛ سرحدی چوکیاں۔ پیشگی اپائنٹمنٹ ضروری ہے (زیادہ مانگ)۔ یہ عمل مفت ہے — جو بھی پیسے مانگے اس پر اعتماد نہ کریں۔ دستاویزات: پاسپورٹ یا شناختی کاغذات (اگر ہوں — بغیر دستاویزات کے بھی درخواست دے سکتے ہیں)۔ اپائنٹمنٹ پر: اپنی درخواست کی وجوہات بتائیں۔ آپ کو 'ارادے کا اظہار' ملے گا جو آپ کو ملک بدری سے بچاتا ہے۔ اس کے بعد: باقاعدہ انٹرویو → سفید فارم (رسید) → سرخ کارڈ (درخواست گزار کی دستاویز)۔

b4-r-fora-termini.text: 1 مہینے کی مدت گزر گئی — لیکن ابھی بھی ممکن ہے
b4-r-fora-termini.note: آپ مدت سے تجاوز کی جائز وجہ پیش کر سکتے ہیں۔ اپنے کیس کی جانچ کے لیے وکیل یا ماہر ادارے (CEAR، SJM، ACNUR) سے رابطہ کریں۔ اگر آپ غیر قانونی حیثیت میں ہیں تو پناہ کی درخواست کارروائی کے دوران ملک بدری کو روک دیتی ہے۔
opts: کیس کی جانچ کے لیے CEAR/SJM/ACNUR سے رابطہ کریں
جائز وجہ کے ساتھ درخواست جمع کرائیں

b4-p-espera.text: آپ کی درخواست کس مرحلے میں ہے؟
b4-p-espera.note: فیصلے کی زیادہ سے زیادہ مدت قبولیت سے 6 مہینے ہے، لیکن عملی طور پر زیادہ وقت لگ سکتا ہے۔ آپ کسی بھی وقت اپنی فائل دیکھ سکتے ہیں۔
opts: قبول ہو گئی، فیصلے کا انتظار
میرے پاس سرخ کارڈ ہے، نظام میں ہوں

b4-r-espera-admissio.text: قبولیت کا انتظار — آپ کے پاس سفید فارم ہے
b4-r-espera-admissio.note: سفید فارم (جمع کرانے کی رسید) انتظار کے دوران آپ کی جائز دستاویز ہے۔ OAR کے پاس قبولیت کے فیصلے کے لیے 1 مہینہ ہے۔ عملی طور پر، تقریباً ہمیشہ خودکار طور پر قبول ہو جاتی ہے۔ اگر 1 مہینے میں اطلاع نہ ملے → درخواست قبول سمجھی جائے گی۔ میعاد ختم ہونے پر دستاویز کی تجدید کریں (سفید فارم کے 9 مہینے بعد)۔
opts: سفید فارم کی میعاد ختم ہونے پر تجدید کی اپائنٹمنٹ لیں (9 مہینے)
اگر مسترد ہو → 2 کاروباری دنوں میں اپیل

b4-r-espera-resolucio.text: درخواست قبول — حتمی فیصلے کا انتظار
b4-r-espera-resolucio.note: زیادہ سے زیادہ مدت: 6 مہینے (عملی طور پر زیادہ لگ سکتا ہے)۔ اگر 6 مہینوں میں فیصلہ نہ ہو تو آپ کو کام کا حق ہے۔ صحت، تعلیم اور پناہ گاہوں تک رسائی (محدود جگہیں)۔ انتظار کے دوران سرخ کارڈ کی سالانہ تجدید کریں۔ آپ OAR سے اپنی فائل کی صورتحال پوچھ سکتے ہیں۔
opts: 6 مہینوں بعد بغیر فیصلے کے کام کا حق
اگر شرائط پوری ہوں تو غیر معمولی regularización 2026
تحفظ مل گیا → مہاجر کی حیثیت

b4-r-tarjeta-roja.text: آپ کے پاس سرخ کارڈ ہے — فعال حیثیت
b4-r-tarjeta-roja.note: سرخ کارڈ ثابت کرتا ہے کہ آپ بین الاقوامی تحفظ کے درخواست گزار ہیں۔ حقوق: صحت، تعلیم، پناہ گاہوں تک رسائی۔ اگر فیصلہ نہ ہو تو 6 مہینوں بعد کام کا حق۔ سالانہ تجدید کریں۔ اگر آپ نے 01/01/2026 سے پہلے پناہ کی درخواست دی ہے تو غیر معمولی regularización 2026 کے لیے درخواست دے سکتے ہیں۔
opts: تحفظ مل گیا → مہاجر کی حیثیت

b4-r-reg-ext.text: غیر معمولی regularización 2026 — پناہ کے درخواست گزاروں کے لیے راستہ
b4-r-reg-ext.note: اگر آپ نے 01/01/2026 سے پہلے بین الاقوامی تحفظ کی درخواست دی ہے تو 30/06/2026 تک غیر معمولی regularización کے لیے درخواست دے سکتے ہیں۔ شرائط: بالغ + اسپین میں + 5 ماہ مسلسل + کوئی مجرمانہ ریکارڈ نہیں + پاسپورٹ/شناختی کاغذ/سفری دستاویز (جائز یا میعاد ختم شدہ)۔ منظوری کی صورت میں → پناہ کی درخواست یا اپیل واپس لینی ہوگی۔ اجازت: 1 سال + کام کا حق۔
opts: 30/06/2026 تک درخواست دیں (آن لائن یا دفتر میں)
سوالات اور اپائنٹمنٹ کے لیے فون 060

b4-p-denegat.text: آپ کو مسترد کی اطلاع کب ملی؟
b4-p-denegat.note: اپیل کی مدت بہت سخت ہے۔ انہیں ضائع نہ ہونے دیں۔
opts: 2 کاروباری دنوں سے کم (قبولیت سے انکار)

b4-r-reexamen.note: اگر آپ کی درخواست قبولیت سے مسترد ہوئی ہے تو نظرثانی کے لیے صرف 2 کاروباری دن ہیں۔ حکام کو 48 گھنٹوں میں جواب دینا ہوگا۔ فوری طور پر وکیل یا ادارے سے رابطہ کریں۔ یہ خدمت مفت ہے۔
opts: سرکاری وکیل: پولیس تھانے میں درخواست دیں

b4-r-recurs.text: فوری — مسترد کے خلاف اپیل: 1 مہینہ
b4-r-recurs.note: آپ کے پاس اطلاع سے 1 مہینہ ہے OAR میں اختیاری نظرثانی کی درخواست کے لیے، یا 2 مہینے عدالتی انتظامی اپیل کے لیے Audiencia Nacional میں۔ بین الاقوامی تحفظ کے ماہر وکیل سے رابطہ کریں۔ اگر آپ کے پاس وسائل نہیں ہیں تو خدمت مفت ہے۔ دیکھیں کہ کیا آپ غیر معمولی regularización 2026 کی شرائط پوری کرتے ہیں (30/06/2026 تک)۔
opts: اگر شرائط پوری ہوں → غیر معمولی regularización 30/06/2026 تک

b4-r-denegat-antic.text: 1 مہینے سے زیادہ پہلے مسترد — باقی ماندہ اختیارات
b4-r-denegat-antic.note: اپیل کی مدت ختم ہو گئی ہے۔ اختیارات: (1) اگر آپ نے 01/01/2026 سے پہلے پناہ کی درخواست دی ہے تو 30/06/2026 تک غیر معمولی regularización کے لیے درخواست دے سکتے ہیں۔ (2) اگر آپ اسپین میں 2 سال سے ہیں تو arraigo کے لیے درخواست دے سکتے ہیں (شاخ 1 دیکھیں)۔ (3) صحت یا ذاتی خطرے کی وجہ سے انسانی تحفظ۔ وکیل یا ماہر ادارے سے مشورہ کریں۔
opts: اسپین میں 2 سال سے ہیں → شاخ 1 arraigo

b4-r-resolucio-positiva.text: مثبت فیصلہ — پناہ گزین کی حیثیت یا ذیلی تحفظ
b4-r-resolucio-positiva.note: مبارک ہو۔ آپ کو یہ حقوق حاصل ہیں: سپین میں رہائش اور کام کا حق، سفری دستاویز (پناہ گزینوں کے لیے)، خاندانی اتحاد، سماجی خدمات تک مساوی رسائی، 5 سال بعد شہریت کی درخواست (پناہ گزینوں کے لیے، عام 10 سال کی بجائے)۔ TIE کارڈ کی وقتاً فوقتاً تجدید ضروری ہے لیکن آپ کی حیثیت مستحکم ہے۔
opts: پناہ گزین کے طور پر TIE کی درخواست کریں
خاندانی اتحاد — توسیع کی درخواست کریں
5 سال میں شہریت (پناہ گزین کی حیثیت)

b1-p0.text: Do you have your passport accessible right now?
b1-p0.note: Without a valid passport, no residence authorization can be processed. This is the first critical fork.
opts: Held by the Immigration Police (Brigada de Extranjería)
I have a national ID from my country but no passport

b1-block-a.text: Passport held by the Provincial Immigration and Borders Brigade
b1-block-a.note: The Brigada holds the passport when there are doubts about the age declared at entry. It can take 6–7 months. You can request a certificate showing the document is being held. Your tutoring entity can follow up.
opts: Is there a criminal case open with the prosecutor?
No, it's an administrative hold → wait

b1-p0b.text: Has the Brigada told you a criminal case has been opened with the prosecutor?
b1-p0b.note: Administrative holding of the passport is common and temporary. If there is also a criminal case for alleged document forgery, the situation is different and requires a lawyer urgently.

b1-sos1-doc.text: Criminal case open over your entry documents
b1-sos1-doc.note: You need a lawyer specialized in criminal law and immigration. Don't try to handle this alone. Court-appointed counsel can be assigned for free if you don't have resources.

b1-block-a-wait.text: Administrative hold — wait for resolution (6–7 months)
b1-block-a-wait.note: While you wait, gather: padrón (city census), health card, school or work reports, certificates from your tutoring entity. When you recover the passport, come back here and continue.
opts: ✓ I have the passport again → continue with P1
Request a hold certificate from the Brigada

b1-block-b.text: Lost or destroyed passport — renew at your country's consulate
b1-block-b.note: Bring the national ID from your country of origin. Senegal, Guinea, Gambia and Benin usually accept renewal with the national ID. Estimated time: 1–3 months. Your entity can accompany you to the consulate.
opts: ✓ I have the new passport → continue with P1
Contact entity for accompaniment to the consulate

b1-block-c.text: No travel document at all — apply for a passport at the consulate
b1-block-c.note: Bring any ID document you have. WARNING: if there is risk of persecution in your country of origin, ask about international protection BEFORE going to the consulate — going may be dangerous.
opts: ⚠ There's risk → redirect to Asylum Branch (B4)
✓ I have the new passport → continue with P1

b1-block-d.text: You have a national ID from your country of origin but no passport
b1-block-d.note: The national ID from your country has no validity in Spain as a travel document. With it you can: register on the padrón, access healthcare and education. You CANNOT process any residence authorization until you have a passport.
opts: Apply for a passport at the consulate using the ID

b1-p1.text: Are you a minor, or did you turn 18 less than 3 months ago?
b1-p1.note: Unaccompanied-minor pathway (art. 174 RD 1155/2024): for those who were under the guardianship of a child protection service and reach adulthood without a residence authorization. Window: 2 months BEFORE up to 3 months AFTER turning 18. Exceptional path: up to age 20 if there are duly documented external causes + a report from the Autonomous Community.
opts: I had guardianship and turned 18 less than 3 months ago
I turned 18 between 3 months and 2 years ago (exceptional path up to 20)
No, I'm an adult without guardianship

b1-r-menor.note: Requirements: be under guardianship, custody or care of a child protection service + have participated in training programmed by the entity (or show integration) + sufficient financial means (IMV or public/private program). Window: 2 months BEFORE up to 3 months AFTER turning 18. Form EX-25. Allows employment by others and self-employment. Administrative silence: UNFAVOURABLE (3 months without response = denied).
opts: Contact the Autonomous Community's child protection service

b1-p2.text: Extraordinary regularization — do you meet the requirements?
b1-p2.note: Window: 16 April – 30 June 2026 (ACTIVE NOW). Requirements: (1) Be in Spain before 1 January 2026. (2) Continuous stay of at least 5 months. (3) No criminal record. NO passport needed — accepts cédula de inscripción or travel title. CANNOT have any other application in process.

b1-r-reg.note: Documents: copy of passport / cédula de inscripción / travel title + proof of being in Spain before 01/01/2026 + proof of 5 months of stay + criminal record certificate (Spain + country of origin). Online channel: available 24h. In-person channel: appointment required. Tel. 060 (9:30–14h and 16:30–19:30h). Once admitted to processing you can already work. Resulting authorization: 1 year.

b1-p3.text: How long have you been living in Spain continuously?
b1-p3.note: You must be able to prove continuous residence: padrón, bills, school reports, health card, social services certificates. If you've been here less than 5 months or arrived after 1/1/2026, the extraordinary regularization doesn't apply either.

b1-r-aviat.text: Too soon — gather documentation for the future
b1-r-aviat.note: Start collecting now: padrón, bills with your address, dated photos, school or social-services reports. When you reach 2 years, come back to the tree.

b1-r-gairebe.text: Almost there — start preparing arraigo social now
b1-r-gairebe.note: Request the integration report from your Autonomous Community now (it can take months). Gather proof of continuous residence. Look for a pre-contract for work if possible.
opts: Request the AC integration report now

b1-p4.text: Do you have an employer willing to give you a work contract?
b1-p4.note: The contract must be at least 90 days and at least 20h/week. Salary must be at least the proportional minimum wage (SMI) for the working hours. Any contract type is accepted. The company must be up-to-date with Social Security.
opts: Yes, I have a contract or pre-contract

b1-r-laboral.note: Requirements: 2 years of continuous residence + active contract of at least 90 days (3 months) and at least 20h/week + minimum salary at proportional SMI. Any contract type accepted: open-ended, temporary, permanent intermittent, replacement. From admission to processing you can already work. Authorization: 1 year. Form EX-10.
opts: From admission to processing → you can already work

b1-p5.text: Are you enrolled, or can you enrol, in official training in Spain?
b1-p5.note: Training valid for arraigo socioformativo: vocational training (FP) middle or higher level, professional certificates (levels 1, 2 or 3), ESO / adult education, SEPE training oriented to employment. NON-official courses or unaccredited academies do NOT count. At most 50% online. You can work 30h/week while studying.
opts: Yes, I'm enrolled or I can enrol

b1-r-formatiu.note: Requirements: 2 years of continuous residence + enrollment in valid official training (FP, professional certificate, adult ESO, SEPE training) + AC social-integration report. You can work 30h/week while studying (self-employed or employee). When you complete the training → modification to a work permit. Form EX-10.

b1-p6.text: Do you have direct family members with legal residence or Spanish nationality?
b1-p6.note: WARNING: Classic arraigo familiar is now for parents of minor EU citizens (not Spanish). If the family member is SPANISH, the procedure is different: it's a 'residence authorization for a family member of a Spanish national' (EX-24), not arraigo. Family members of legal residents (spouse/partner or 1st-degree direct-line relative) go via arraigo social with proof of financial means.
opts: Yes, child or parent of a Spaniard
Yes, spouse or partner of a legal resident
Yes, parent of a minor EU citizen (non-Spanish)

b1-r-familiar.text: Arraigo social via family ties (spouse/partner of legal resident)
b1-r-familiar.note: Requirements: 2 years of continuous residence + proven family bond (spouse or registered domestic partner, or 1st-degree direct-line relative, with legal residence) + financial means at 200% IPREM (~€1,200/month). Form EX-10. Note: if the family member is SPANISH, see EX-24 option.

b1-p7.text: Do you have, or can you get, a social-integration report from your Autonomous Community?
b1-p7.note: You request the integration report from the AC where you live (social services or equivalent body). In Catalonia: Departament de Drets Socials. In Madrid: Comunidad de Madrid. It can take 2–6 months. Request it well in advance. It certifies: time of stay, language knowledge, participation in social activities.
opts: Yes, I have it or can request it

b1-r-social.note: Requirements: 2 years of continuous residence + AC integration report (or family ties with legal residents) + own or family financial means equivalent to 100% IPREM. Allows self-employment or employment by others. Form EX-10.
opts: Request the AC integration report now (takes months)

b1-p8.text: Does your situation involve immediate risk? (detention, expulsion, police)
b1-p8.note: If you're being held right now or have an imminent expulsion notice, activate SOS mode. If the situation is stable but you have no pathway, contact an entity.

b1-sos2.note: You have the right to free legal assistance. You have the right to an interpreter. You should not sign any document without reading and understanding it. You can ask that your lawyer or reference entity be contacted.
opts: Show this screen to the police

b1-r-sense.text: No active pathway right now — accompaniment and preparation
b1-r-sense.note: No active pathway right now for your profile. Contact a reference entity: CEAR, SJM, Red Cross or local entity. Gather residence documentation. If your situation changes (work, training, family), come back to the tree. The 'second-chance' arraigo does NOT apply if you've never had a residence authorization.

b1-r-familiar-espanyol.text: Residence authorization as family member of a Spanish national
b1-r-familiar-espanyol.note: Child of a Spanish parent of origin (no age limit), spouse or partner of a Spaniard, dependent ascendant/descendant. Does NOT require minimum residence time in Spain. Initial authorization 5 years. Form EX-24.

b1-r-familiar-ue.text: Arraigo familiar — parent of a non-Spanish minor EU citizen
b1-r-familiar-ue.note: For parents of a minor EU/EEA/Swiss (non-Spanish) child. You must have the minor in your care and live with them. Form EX-10.

b1-r-menor-excepcional.text: Exceptional minor pathway — up to age 20
b1-r-menor-excepcional.note: If the ordinary window (3 months after turning 18) has passed but you haven't yet turned 20, you can apply exceptionally if you prove duly documented external causes + a report from the Autonomous Community or municipality. URGENTLY contact the entity that had your guardianship or a lawyer specialized in minors.

b2-p0.text: What type of authorization do you have or did you have?
b2-p0.note: Look at your card (TIE) or the resolution. The type is stated on the document.
opts: I have an authorization but it has expired
I had an authorization but lost it (irregular now)
I don't know what type I have

b2-r-identificar.text: Identify your document — bring it to an entity
b2-r-identificar.note: Bring your physical document (TIE or resolution) to a reference entity or immigration office. They'll be able to tell you exactly what type of authorization you have and what options you have.

b2-p1.text: When does your temporary residence authorization expire?
b2-p1.note: Official window: from 60 days BEFORE up to 90 days AFTER expiry. With the application filed on time, validity is extended until resolution (max. 3 months). Administrative silence is FAVOURABLE on renewal.

b2-r-ok.text: You have time — prepare the renewal now
b2-r-ok.note: Submit between 60 days before and the expiry date. Approved renewal lasts 2 years. Base documents: relevant EX form, valid passport, padrón, criminal-record certificate + documents specific to your permit type. Online (Mercurio) or in person with appointment.

b2-r-urgent.text: URGENT — less than 2 months to expiry
b2-r-urgent.note: Submit the application THIS WEEK. With the application filed, validity is extended until resolution. Administrative silence FAVOURABLE: if no response in 3 months, it's deemed approved.

b2-r-critic.text: CRITICAL — expires in less than 1 month
b2-r-critic.note: Very urgent situation. Submit the application today if possible. With the application filed, even out of window, a positive resolution will regularize your situation. Get help from a lawyer or specialized entity.

b2-r-ld.note: Long-term residence is indefinite. The TIE must be renewed every 5 years (simple procedure, doesn't reopen the merits). You have full labour rights, can sponsor family reunification and time counts toward nationality.

b2-p2.text: How long ago did your authorization expire?
b2-p2.note: Past 90 days from expiry without filing, ordinary renewal is no longer possible.
opts: Less than 90 days → you can still renew

b2-r-caducada-recent.text: Expired less than 90 days ago — you can still renew
b2-r-caducada-recent.note: Legally you can file up to 90 days AFTER expiry. Note: you are in irregular status (minor offence if <3 months, serious if >3 months). File today. A penalty file may be opened, but an approved renewal regularizes the situation.
opts: Get support from an entity or lawyer

b2-r-caducada-mitja.text: Expired between 90 days and 1 year — serious irregularity
b2-r-caducada-mitja.note: Ordinary renewal no longer possible. Serious irregular status (fine or expulsion file possible). Options: (1) 2 years in Spain → arraigo Branch 1. (2) Authorization in last 2 years → second-chance arraigo. (3) Extraordinary regularization until 30/06/2026 if you meet requirements.

b2-r-caducada-llarga.text: Expired more than 1 year ago — complex situation
b2-r-caducada-llarga.note: Ordinary renewal is probably not viable. Options depend on the specific situation: if you've been in Spain 2+ years you can opt for arraigo (see Branch 1). If you had authorization in the last 2 years, you can opt for second-chance arraigo.
opts: Authorization in last 2 years → second-chance arraigo

b2-p3.text: When did you lose or stop having the authorization?
b2-p3.note: Second-chance arraigo requires having had a residence authorization in the 2 years immediately before the application.

b2-r-2a-op.note: For someone who had a residence authorization (NOT for exceptional circumstances, i.e. not a previous arraigo) in the last 2 years and couldn't renew for reasons OTHER than public order or security. Requirements: 2 years of continuous residence + no criminal record. EXCLUDES active asylum applicants and those coming from a previous arraigo. Authorization: 1 year + work. Form EX-10.
opts: Was the previous authorization an arraigo? → doesn't apply

b2-r-antiga.text: Authorization more than 2 years ago — second-chance arraigo unavailable
b2-r-antiga.note: Second-chance arraigo requires the authorization to have been in the last 2 years. If it's older, this pathway isn't available. Options: if you've been in Spain 2+ years, look at Branch 1 arraigo pathways (sociolaboral, socioformativo, social).

b3-p0.text: Are you a citizen of the EU, EEA or Switzerland?
b3-p0.note: EU: Germany, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Denmark, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Estonia, Finland, France, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Sweden, Czech Republic, Cyprus. EEA also: Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway. Switzerland by bilateral agreement.
opts: No, I'm a non-EU family member of an EU citizen
I'm a family member of a Spanish national

b3-p1.text: How long have you been in Spain or do you plan to stay?
b3-p1.note: EU freedom of movement allows up to 90 days with no procedure. From day 91 onwards, registration is mandatory.

b3-r-curta.note: Up to 90 days you can stay in Spain freely with your passport or national ID. You don't need to register or get any document. If you want to work, you need the NIE for tax purposes, but it's not mandatory for the stay.
opts: If you'll work → request NIE at the immigration office

b3-p2.text: Have you registered at the Central Registry of Foreigners (green certificate)?
b3-p2.note: The green certificate (NIE verde / CUE) is mandatory if you reside more than 3 months. It must be requested in the first 3 months from entry. Obtained at the immigration office or police station of your province. Form EX-18. Issued same day.

b3-r-registre.text: You must register — get the green certificate (NIE verde)
b3-r-registre.note: Mandatory if you stay or will stay more than 3 months. Window: first 3 months from entry. Where: immigration office or police station of your province. Appointment required (high demand, book early). Documents: form EX-18 + valid passport/ID + documents justifying the residence reason (contract, enrollment, financial means + health insurance). Fee: 790 code 012. The certificate is issued same day. Doesn't expire but residence must be maintained.
opts: Employee → work contract or company declaration
No activity → sufficient financial means + health insurance

b3-p3.text: How long have you been legally residing in Spain?
b3-p3.note: Permanent residence is acquired with 5 years of continuous legal residence. Interruptions of less than 2 consecutive years don't affect the count.

b3-r-permanent.note: With 5 years of continuous legal residence you're entitled to permanent residence. The EU permanent residence certificate is obtained at the immigration office or police station. Form EX-18. Validity: 10 years, automatically renewable. Rights: work, social services, healthcare, family reunification, option to nationality (10 years total legal residence, reduced to 2 years for Ibero-Americans and other countries with agreements).
opts: Start nationality process if you have 10 years

b3-p4.text: What is your main goal right now?
opts: Bring family from outside the EU

b3-r-treball.text: Work in Spain as an EU citizen
b3-r-treball.note: You have free access to the Spanish labour market. With the green certificate (NIE verde) you can work without any additional permit. You need the NIE for tax and labour matters. The company must register you with Social Security. If self-employed: register with the tax authority (form 036/037) + register with SS.
opts: Have the green certificate? → you can work

b3-r-familia.text: Bring family from outside the EU — community regime
b3-r-familia.note: Non-EU family members (spouse, children <21 or dependent, dependent ascendants, registered partner) have the right to accompany you or reunify. The community regime is more favourable than the general regime. They must apply for the EU citizen family-member residence card. Form EX-19. Validity: 5 years. Application window: 90 days from entry to Spain. Cost: fee 790 code 012 (equivalent to a Spanish DNI).
opts: Family member files EX-19 within 90 days of entry
Spanish spouse? → EX-19 if they exercised free movement, EX-24 otherwise

b3-r-estudis.text: Study in Spain as an EU citizen
b3-r-estudis.note: You have the right to study under equal conditions as Spaniards (prices, scholarships, access). You need the green certificate if the stay is longer than 3 months. Documents for registration: form EX-18 + enrollment from the educational center + sufficient financial means (show you won't be a burden on the state) + public or private health insurance with adequate coverage.

b3-r-serveis.text: Access to healthcare and social services as an EU citizen
b3-r-serveis.note: With the green certificate and contributing to Social Security you have full access to public healthcare. If you don't work, you must show financial means and health insurance. Padrón registration gives you access to municipal and regional services. Individual health card: request it at the CAP or health centre in your area.
opts: Padrón + green certificate → access to services

b3-p-familiar.text: What is your relationship with the EU citizen?
b3-p-familiar.note: The community regime is very favourable for family members. The application must be made within 90 days from entry to Spain. Form EX-19.

b3-r-familiar-ue.note: Apply within 90 days from entry. Documents: form EX-19 + valid passport + documentation of family bond (apostilled and translated marriage/birth certificate) + green NIE of the EU family member. Cost: fee 790 code 012 (DNI price). Initial validity: 5 years. After 5 years → permanent residence (10 years, automatically renewable). Form EX-19.

b3-r-familiar-altres.note: Other family members (siblings, in-laws, nephews/nieces...) don't have automatic right to the community regime. You must show economic dependence or 24 months of cohabitation in the country of origin. The decision is discretionary at the immigration office. We recommend a lawyer specialized in EU law.
opts: Consult a lawyer specialized in EU law

b3-r-familiar-espanyol.text: Family of a Spanish national — two possible pathways
b3-r-familiar-espanyol.note: If your Spanish family member has exercised the right to free movement (lived in another EU country), you can go via EX-19 (community regime, very favourable). If they haven't exercised free movement, you must go via EX-24 (general regime for family of a Spaniard). When in doubt, consult a lawyer — the EX-19 pathway is much more favourable and worth exploring.
opts: Has lived in another EU country → EX-19 (community)
Has never lived outside Spain → EX-24 (general)
Consult a lawyer to determine best pathway

b4-p0.note: The place where you are determines how you must proceed. You have the right to apply for international protection regardless of where you are.
opts: At the Spanish border, port or airport

b4-r-frontera.text: RIGHT TO REQUEST ASYLUM AT THE BORDER — Show this screen
b4-r-frontera.note: You have the right to request international protection at any entry point. Say clearly: 'I request international protection'. They cannot expel you until the application is processed. You have the right to a free interpreter. You have the right to a free lawyer. The application is CONFIDENTIAL — it's not communicated to your country of origin. If your application is not admitted for processing, you have 2 working days to file a re-examination.

b4-r-cie.text: In a CIE — you can request asylum from here
b4-r-cie.note: You can request international protection even while detained in a CIE. Ask to speak with the duty lawyer or contact UNHCR or CEAR. You have the right to a free interpreter and lawyer. The application halts the expulsion procedure while it's being processed.

b4-p1.text: Have you already filed the international protection application?
b4-p1.note: The window for asylum is 1 month from entry to Spain. If you've passed the month, you can show justified cause to extend the window. The procedure is free.

b4-p-nosol.text: Have you been in Spain for less than 1 month?
b4-p-nosol.note: Official window is 1 month from entry. After the window, you can claim justified cause. The procedure is free and confidential.

b4-r-com-sol.text: How to request international protection in Spain
b4-r-com-sol.note: Where to file: Asylum and Refuge Office (OAR) — Madrid: C/ Pradillo 40; Police stations with immigration service (any province); Border posts. Appointment required (high demand). The procedure is FREE — beware of anyone charging. Documents: passport or ID document (if you have one — applications without documents are also possible). At the appointment: explain the reasons for your application. They'll give you a 'Manifestación de voluntad' that protects you from expulsion. Then: formal interview → white sheet (receipt) → red card (applicant document).

b4-r-fora-termini.text: Outside the 1-month window — but still possible
b4-r-fora-termini.note: You can claim justified cause to exceed the window. Contact a lawyer or specialized entity (CEAR, SJM, UNHCR) to assess your case. If you're in irregular status, an asylum application halts any expulsion file while it's processed.
opts: Contact CEAR/SJM/UNHCR to assess the case

b4-p-espera.text: What stage is your application at?
b4-p-espera.note: The maximum resolution window is 6 months from admission to processing, but in practice it can take much longer. You have the right to consult your file at any time.
opts: I have the red card, I'm in the system

b4-r-espera-admissio.text: Awaiting admission to processing — you have the white sheet
b4-r-espera-admissio.note: The white sheet (Filing Receipt) is your valid document while you wait. The OAR has 1 month to decide whether to admit it for processing. In practice, it's almost always admitted automatically. If you don't get a notification within 1 month → the application is deemed admitted. Renew the document when it expires (9 months after the white sheet).
opts: Renew appointment when the white sheet expires (9 months)
If not admitted → appeal within 2 working days

b4-r-espera-resolucio.note: Maximum window: 6 months (can take much longer in practice). You have the right to work 6 months after the application if you've received no resolution. Access to healthcare, education and the reception system (limited spots). Renew the red card annually while you wait. You can check the status of your file at the OAR.
opts: Right to work 6 months without resolution
2026 extraordinary regularization if you meet requirements

b4-r-tarjeta-roja.text: You have the red card — active status
b4-r-tarjeta-roja.note: The red card certifies you're an international-protection applicant. Rights: access to healthcare, education, reception system. Right to work 6 months after the application if there's no resolution. Renew it annually. If you applied for asylum before 01/01/2026, you can opt for the 2026 extraordinary regularization.

b4-r-reg-ext.text: 2026 extraordinary regularization — pathway for asylum applicants
b4-r-reg-ext.note: If you applied for international protection BEFORE 01/01/2026, you can opt for the extraordinary regularization until 30/06/2026. Requirements: adult + in Spain + 5 continuous months + no criminal record + passport/cédula/travel title (valid or expired). If approved → you must withdraw the asylum application or the appeal. Authorization: 1 year + right to work.

b4-p-denegat.text: When were you notified of the denial?
b4-p-denegat.note: Appeal windows are very strict. Don't miss them.

b4-r-reexamen.note: If you weren't admitted to processing, you have ONLY 2 working days to file a re-examination. Authorities have 48 hours to respond. Contact a lawyer or entity IMMEDIATELY. The service is free.
opts: Court-appointed lawyer: request at the police station

b4-r-recurs.note: You have 1 month from notification to file an optional appeal for reconsideration before the OAR, or 2 months for a contentious-administrative appeal before the National Court. Contact a lawyer specialized in international protection. The service is free if you don't have resources. Consider whether you met requirements for the 2026 extraordinary regularization (until 30/06/2026).
opts: Extraordinary regularization if it applies → until 30/06/2026

b4-r-denegat-antic.text: Denial more than 1 month ago — residual options
b4-r-denegat-antic.note: Appeal windows have passed. Options: (1) If you applied for asylum before 01/01/2026, you can opt for the extraordinary regularization until 30/06/2026. (2) If you've been in Spain 2 years, you can opt for arraigo (see Branch 1). (3) Humanitarian protection on health or personal-risk grounds. Consult a lawyer or specialized entity.

b4-r-resolucio-positiva.text: Favourable resolution — Refugee status or subsidiary protection
b4-r-resolucio-positiva.note: Congratulations. You have the right to: residence and work in Spain, travel document (refugees), family reunification, equal access to social services, nationality application after 5 years (refugees, instead of the usual 10). The card must be renewed periodically but the status is stable.
opts: Apply for TIE as a refugee
